expectations.text: Primary keyword 'black pepper' and 'turmeric' both appear in the first paragraph
expectations.evidence: Opening mentions 'Long before bioavailability was a word... cooks were grinding black pepper and turmeric together'

expectations.text: Body contains at least 4 Markdown internal links [text](/path)
expectations.evidence: Only 1 Markdown link in body prose ([curcumin](/ingredients/turmeric)). 'Related Ingredients' and 'Recipes to Try' sections at bottom have NO links — just plain text bullet points.

expectations.text: YAML frontmatter has title, slug, tags, and meta_description
Body does not open with a # H1 heading (page template renders the title)
expectations.evidence: Line 19: '# Why Black Pepper Makes Turmeric Work Better: The Science of Piperine and Curcumin' — duplicate H1 that will conflict with page template

expectations.text: Post includes a FAQ section with at least 2 questions
expectations.evidence: No FAQ section. Post ends with 'The Larger Lesson' and 'Related Ingredients' / 'Recipes to Try' sections instead.

expectations.text: Post does not end with a boilerplate 'Ancient Pantry presents...' disclaimer
expectations.evidence: Line 160: 'At Ancient Pantry, we explore the intersection of traditional ingredient wisdom and modern food science. Traditional claims represent historical systems of knowledge...'

expectations.text: YAML includes an internal_links_used audit field
Health claims use qualified language (research suggests, associated with) — no unqualified 'heals' or 'cures'
expectations.evidence: Uses 'Research suggests', 'has been associated', 'clinical trials show'. No unqualified claims.
